title: check_tone
description: Score an outgoing message on three tone axes (directness, warmth, urgency) on a 0..100 scale, optionally relative to a baseline of the sender's prior messages, and flag phrases whose tone deviates meaningfully from baseline or from the target register. Does NOT rewrite; pair with rewrite_outgoing for that. Server orchestrates a structured prompt against the caller's MCP-client LLM; the server itself performs NO LLM calls.

text.description: The outgoing message to score. Stored only in-memory for the duration of the call; never logged.
examples: This is broken. Please fix before EOD.
Just circling back on the spec review — any chance you've had a look?
Strong nack. The approach in section 3 will not scale and we need to revisit before merge.

baseline_messages.description: Optional sample of the same sender's prior messages in this thread or channel. Used to compute baseline_delta — how much this message's tone diverges from the sender's usual register. Capped at 20 items. When absent or fewer than 3 items, baseline_delta is null in the output.
examples: Thanks for the writeup — really useful framing.
Happy to pair on this tomorrow if helpful.
Good catch on the migration edge case; let's land that fix first.

target_register.description: Optional target register against which to score the message. When present, axes_target is populated and suggested_rewrite_hint is keyed off the gap. When absent, the analysis is descriptive only.

channel.description: Optional channel hint. Influences register expectations (slack is informal, email is formal, github lean technical/blunt). When absent the prompt assumes 'generic'.

axes.description: Absolute tone scores on three axes, each 0..100. Calibration: 50 is neutral; <30 is notably low; >70 is notably high. Calibration anchors are documented in the eval corpus README.

axes_target.description: Tone scores corresponding to the target_register, when target_register was supplied. Null otherwise. These are the values the rewrite would aim for.

baseline_delta.description: Signed percentage-point deltas vs the baseline_messages sample. Null when baseline_messages was absent or fewer than 3 items were supplied. Positive means this message is more X than baseline; negative means less. Per plan.md §7 acceptance, phrases with thread-baseline delta > 25 percentage points are flagged.

flagged_phrases.description: Phrases whose tone diverges from baseline by more than 25 percentage points on any axis, OR which read as substantially off-target relative to target_register. Ordered by severity desc. Empty array when nothing is flagged.

suggested_rewrite_hint.description: Short prose hint describing the direction a rewrite should take (e.g. 'soften the directness, preserve the technical correction'). Null when no rewrite is recommended. This is a hint — the actual rewrite happens via rewrite_outgoing.

eval_corpus_slice.description: Identifier of the eval corpus slice that validates this prompt template. Required so that any prompt change can be re-evaluated in CI per plan.md §7.

model_provenance.description: Where the LLM call ran (local or cloud, model id). Surfaced so the user / extension can render the 'cloud mode' banner accurately.

TEXT_REQUIRED.description: The text argument was missing or empty.

TEXT_TOO_LONG.description: Input text exceeded the 8000-character cap. Caller SHOULD split or excerpt before calling.

BASELINE_TOO_SMALL.description: Soft signal, not a hard error: fewer than 3 baseline_messages were supplied. The server returns axes but sets baseline_delta to null. Callers MAY ignore.

TARGET_REGISTER_UNKNOWN.description: target_register was not one of: direct, warm, formal, concise, clarifying.

MODEL_UNAVAILABLE.description: The caller's MCP client could not reach its configured LLM.

examples.name: blunt PR comment on a thread of warm baseline messages

input.text: Strong nack. The approach in section 3 will not scale and we need to revisit before merge.
baseline_messages: Thanks for the writeup — really useful framing.
Happy to pair on this tomorrow if helpful.
Good catch on the migration edge case; let's land that fix first.

flagged_phrases.note: Opens with a blunt rejection token; reads as a 58 percentage-point drop in warmth vs this sender's baseline. Considered substantially blunter-than-baseline.
Strong assertion with no hedge; readers may parse as a dismissal of the author's work rather than a technical concern.

output.suggested_rewrite_hint: Preserve the technical objection and the merge-blocker signal, but open with one sentence of acknowledgement and replace 'Strong nack' with a phrase that names the specific concern (e.g. 'I think section 3 is going to hit a scaling limit — can we revisit before merge?').

examples.name: polite follow-up against unknown baseline (descriptive only)

input.text: Just circling back on the spec review — any chance you've had a look?

compatibility.policy: Additive-only within v0.1.x. New optional axes (e.g. formality) are minor bumps. Renaming or retyping axes requires a new major version with a parallel $id. The 25 percentage-point flagging threshold is an internal heuristic, not part of the wire contract, and may be tuned within v0.1.x as the eval corpus grows.
compatibility.side_effects: None. Pure read. The server stores no input and emits no telemetry.
compatibility.idempotency: Trivially idempotent on the server. LLM-side determinism depends on the caller's model and sampling settings.
compatibility.privacy: local-only-by-default. The server itself does not call any provider. Cloud-mode is selected by the caller's MCP client and the user's profile; this server is provider-agnostic.

TargetRegister.description: Target register the rewrite should aim for. 'direct' = unhedged but not aggressive; 'warm' = relational, acknowledges person; 'formal' = professional, third-person friendly; 'concise' = minimum viable words; 'clarifying' = framed around surfacing missing information.

directness.description: Unhedged-ness. 0 = entirely hedged / softened; 100 = blunt, unmitigated assertion.

warmth.description: Relational warmth. 0 = cold, transactional; 100 = effusively friendly.

urgency.description: Time pressure signalled. 0 = no time pressure; 100 = drop-everything urgency.

start_char.description: Zero-indexed inclusive character offset into input.text.

end_char.description: Zero-indexed exclusive character offset. end_char > start_char.

phrase.description: Verbatim slice of input.text between the offsets, for caller convenience.

axis.description: Which axis this phrase moves significantly.

delta.description: Estimated contribution of this phrase to the baseline_delta or target gap on the named axis, in percentage points.

note.description: Optional short prose explanation of why this phrase was flagged.
